title: Flickingeria annamensis
taxonomy: Flickingeria annamensis is classified as a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It belongs to the genus Flickingeria. The plant is native to Vietnam and is considered an endangered species due to habitat loss and degradation. The exact classification details of Flickingeria annamensis are as follows: it is a member of the subtribe Aeridinae and tribe Aeridinae. This orchid species is highly valued for its unique and delicate flower shape.

intro: Flickingeria annamensis is a species of orchid native to Southeast Asia, known for its delicate white flowers and slender leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Flickingeria annamensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
